item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: There seems to be a small error in the 255 crosslink, as 3214 Hybris is an asteroid, not a star. Didn't want to fix it myself, though, just in case I was missing something.

item.title: Staff Post - Open
item.content:encoded:
The image that was used in this article has been replaced with the following image, which is cc-compliant with the SCP wiki's license (CC BY-SA 3.0):

Source: SCP Foundation Wiki
License: CC BY-SA 3.0
Title: 1574.jpg
Author: Roget
Release year: 2013

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Only question is why did it stay still as a fence if it usually flies randomly?

item.title: Staff Post - Open
item.content:encoded:
Timer Expired. Edits made as discussed below. Author may revert at anytime.

The WikiWalk team is proposing edits to this page. All proposed edits will be in a collapsible below this post, and after 7 days if there are no serious objections to the edits, the feedback received will be taken into account as the edits are made. If you have questions not directly related to the article about this project, please pm a member of the WikiWalk Team

+ Show Block
- Hide Block

Skips that contain references to other skips, but have the latter's number blackboxed are great for adding crosslinks, since a spot for a link is already built into the article, you just need to find a suitable object to fill in the blank. With respect to the "SCP-███" refrence here, Roget claimed that any suitable keter anomaly would work. For me, I like the idea of using SCP-255 here since its both keter, extraterrestrial, and capable of doing the damage described in the log. As such, I am recommending the following edit:

Changing this:
09/18/2012 Appeared identical to SCP-███, which had been contained in the same area SCP-1574 had manifested in. Displayed anomalous properties identical to SCP-███, resulting in ██ casualties to observation team.
To this:
09/18/2012 Appeared as a miniature version of the star 3214 Hybris, which had been studied in the same area SCP-1574 had manifested in. Displayed anomalous properties identical to SCP-255, resulting in ██ casualties to observation team.

If anyone has other/better examples that could be used here please leave a comment saying so!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Yeah, I'd kind of figure that the [DATA EXPUNGED] manifesting on [REDACTED] would be more of a prime reason for this to be keter. If it didn't omit a dangerous scp being copied, I'm starting to wonder what would be less worse but still warrant the specific details being wiped.

Memetics, maybe?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I like this a lot, but the redacted parts don't add anything to the story at all.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: had me until the end part, when it just became a random thing prophesying current events on the day it happens

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Ah, well that's sort of similar to this one, although I'd imagine the "broken cloak" trope has been passed around for quite awhile, beyond dr. who.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Basically, the Tardis (Dr. Who's vehicle) is supposed to have a mechanism which somehow manipulates the perceptions of it, and is supposed to always disguise itself as something innocuous fo the environment. A running joke in the series is that it's stuck on the setting for the iconic blue police-box, which sticks out like a sore thumb. Every once in a while, they fix the Circuit, but it always returns to status quo.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Never seen it, since I don't watch that show. Care to explain what it is, in plain terms that my weak, non-whovian brain can comprehend?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Perception-altering effect, nice. I can't help but get a Doctor Who "Chameleon Circuit" feel from it.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Well, this is better than immobile object / unstoppable force, two other floating spheres. There are already transmissions from dying alien races. I'm not feeling it, but it's salvageable. No vote.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Or old man Jenkins with a rubber mask?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: What? It was never a weather balloon or swamp gas?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Heh, that "perception-altering effect" is neat. +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I actually though Rhett was the author, it being a Keter sphere and all…

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: It just doesn't do anything for me.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: That makes an incredible amount of sense.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Because, if it's like in my headcanon, all objects are Euclid first, then declassified to Safe or upgraded to Keter.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Also:

Addendum 11574-A:

Is that extra "1" supposed to be there?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Any changes to its appearance is to be logged in addition to changes in location.

Verb tense

approximately 1.63 meters in diameter

This bothers me.

SCP-1574 was documented as an SCP on 11/23/1941, and classified as Euclid.

Why was this ever Euclid?

Anyways, I'm not feeling this one.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I had all but the Euclid fixed by the time you posted the comment, it's fixed now.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
constant speed of 20 k/ph

and classified as Euclid.

The striking makes no sense in this context.

Addendum 11574-A: Changes to SCP-1574's appearance

Should be messages.

I'm neutral on the concept.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Excellent. The imitation angle is the one I was hoping you were going for. Would upvote again.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: That was how they realized it can go anywhere and presumably imitate any anomalous object on earth. Imagine if it began to go towards a stationary keter site…

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Melikes. Only question I have is, what caused them to reclassify it as Keter, specifically? The transmission from the Agent? I don't question it being Keter, just wondering what the rationale was.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Upvoted for the Barrack Obama head. The rest was pretty cool too, I guess.
